ANSWER TO COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 16189012) FILED BY DEFENDANT INTERNET ARCHIVE OPEN LIBRARY OF SAN FRANCISCO

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT INTERNET ARCHIVE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE ANSWER FILED ON JULY 8 COMPLIES WITH THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING ISSUED IN ADVANCE OF THE HEARING SET FOR JULY 7. THE COURT RE-AFFIRMS THAT TENTATIVE RULING THAT DEFENDANTS' MOTION SHOULD BE GRANTED BECAUSE THEY HAVE SUBMITTED SUFFICIENT FACT FOR RELIEF UNDER BOTH THE MANDATORY AND DISCRETIONARY PROVISIONS OF CCP 437(B). WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: LAURA MARTINEZ, CSR #11332, BUS. TEL: 415-551-3745. (D302)

MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOT TO SET ASIDE (TRANSACTION ID # 16152053) FILED BY DEFENDANT INTERNET ARCHIVE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT INTERNET ARCHIVE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS CONTINUED FROM JULY 7, 2016 TO JULY 26, 2016 AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE THE MOVING PARTY AN OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENT OF CCP 473(B) TO FILE A PROPOSED RESPONSIVE PLEADING. NO LATER THAN JULY 14, 2016 THE MOVING PARTY MUST FILE AND SERVE A PROPOSED RESPONSIVE PLEADING. IF THAT IS NOT DONE, THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. IF A PROPOSED RESPONSIVE PLEADING IS FILED AND SERVED BY JULY 14, 2016, THEN PLAINTIFF MAY FILE SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY PAPERS DIRECTED SOLELY TO THE ISSUE OF THE SUFFICIENCY OF THE PROPOSED RESPONSIVE PLEADING. FOR THE GUIDANCE OF THE PARTIES, BUT FOR THE MOVING PARTY'S FAILURE TO FILE A PROPOSED RESPONSIVE PLEADING, THE TENTATIVE RULING WOULD BE TO GRANT THE RULING ON THE GROUNDS THAT BOTH THE MANDATORY AND DISCRETIONARY RELIEF PROVISIONS OF SECTION 437(B) HAVE BEEN MET AND CALIFORNIA'S STRONG POLICY OF RESOLVING CASES ON THEIR MERITS. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =302/HK
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-26-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT INTERNET ARCHIVE, OPEN LIBRARY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-15-548826 WITH CASE CGC-14-542560 IS DENIED. GRANTING THE MOTION WOULD BE PREJUDICIAL TO MR. PICARELLO BECAUSE IT WOULD SIGNIFICANTLY DELAY HIS TRIAL DATE AND LIKELY CAUSE HIM TO INCUR ADDITIONAL COSTS RELATING TO DISCOVERY. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-24-2017 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-05-2016 Set For Trial On Apr-24-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-15-548826 WITH CASE CGC-14-542560
Off Calendar Sep-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Thomas Picarello (415.623.9483) appears as opposing party/plaintiff In Propria Persona in related action CGC-15-548826 (Picarello vs Internet Archive)

Attorney, Heidi Adams of Kuluva Armijo & Garcia Law Offices (415.273.6500) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Internet Archive

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT INTERNET ARCHIVE, OPEN LIBRARY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-15-548826 WITH CASE CGC-14-542560 is DENIED. Granting the motion would be prejudicial to Mr. Picarello because it would significantly delay his trial date and likely cause him to incur additional costs relating to discovery. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-21-2016 Continued To Oct-19-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: Appearance by Emmanuel Enyinwa, Esq. for Plaintiff. A hearing on a motion to consolidate is pending. A Case Management Conference is set for Oct-19-2016, at 10:30 AM in Dept. 610 for submission of CMC Statments. JUDGE JOHN K. STEWART; Clerk RWoods. Proceedings not reported. (610)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Continued To Sep-20-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Internet Archive'S Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Jul-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Kenyon M. Young of Kuluva Armijo & Garcia (415-273-6503) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

Defendant Internet Archive's Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment is GRANTED. The answer filed on July 8 complies with the court's tentative ruling issued in advance of the hearing set for July 7. The court re-affirms that tentative ruling that defendants' motion should be granted because they have submitted sufficient fact for relief under both the mandatory and discretionary provisions of CCP 437(b). Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Internet Archive's motion to set aside default is continued to July 26, 2016 to give the moving party an opportunity to comply with the requirement of CCP 473(b) to file a proposed responsive pleading. No later than July 14 the moving party must file and serve a proposed responsive pleading. If that is not done, the motion will be denied. If a proposed responsive pleading is filed and served by July 14, then plaintiff may file supplemental reply papers directed solely to the issue of the sufficiency of the proposed responsive pleading. For the guidance of the parties, but for the moving party's failure to file a proposed responsive pleading, the tentative ruling would be to grant the ruling on the grounds that both the mandatory and discretionary relief provisions of section 437(b) have been met and California's strong policy of resolving cases on their merits. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
